title: Add Renode nRF52840 boot+TX validation to CI matrix
description: Bead project-LICHEN-r7h4.5 validated Renode nRF52840 boot, SPI, L2 init and LoRa TX end-to-end on Renode 1.16.1 (linux-arm64). Add a CI job that builds the T-Echo puck firmware with the Renode console overlay and runs a headless boot+TX smoke test. Current .github/workflows/renode.yml only validates that .repl/.resc parse and runs the no-firmware bridge tests; it uses the x86_64 portable Renode and does not build firmware. Extend it (or add a job) to: install matching Renode, install the Zephyr SDK/west workspace, build t_echo with the renode_console overlay/conf (needs -DBOARD_ROOT, -DZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=lichen, SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH), and assert boot + transmissions>0. Hold RX assertions until project-LICHEN-r7h4.6/.7 land.
acceptance_criteria: CI job builds t_echo firmware and asserts Renode boot + LoRa TX to lichen-sim; green on main.
close_reason: Fixed: Added renode-firmware CI job that builds t_echo puck with renode_console overlay and runs test_mesh.py boot+TX validation against lichen-sim (2 nodes, 120s timeout). Uses same Renode 1.16.1 and Zephyr caching as existing CI jobs.
